corrections.reason: D01_INCENDIE not supported: constats_body is entirely about défrichement/chiroptères/Capricorne, no incendie signal.

error_patterns: 1) m_DELAI over-application (24 records): Hunter attached m_DELAI whenever a fiche contained a 'Demande à formuler'/'Observations' section, even when the demand carried no numeric délai ('dès réception', 'lors de la prochaine campagne', 'mérite d'être', or no demand at all). Verified systematically with a regex over constats_body for délai signals (sous/dans X jours|mois|semaine, délai de, d'ici au, avant le, au plus tard) cross-checked against every m_DELAI-tagged record in the batch, then manually confirmed each flagged case. No case of the inverse error (numeric delay present but m_DELAI missing) was confirmed after removing false positives from an imprecise regex pass. 2) D03_SEVESO bleed across sibling Lafarge-carrière fiches (2 records): applied to GEREP/plan-transmission fiches with zero Seveso/EDD/MMR/PPRT content, likely carried over from other fiches in the same inspection report that do legitimately discuss Seveso topics (e.g. laffort f09, correctly tagged). 3) Actor mislabeling (3 records): A02_INSPECTION or A06_BET assigned as principal actor when the exploitant remains the subject of the finding and the inspection/BET is only a secondary mentioned party. 4) Reasoning/value swap between sibling fiches (1 record, lg-saucats f10/f11): Hunter's stated reasoning for f10 actually describes f11's piézomètre content; f10 itself is a fully conforming eaux-de-surface finding, but was tagged with f11's NC-oriented mechanisms/dynamic. 5) M14_CONSTAT_TECHNIQUE over-labeling on administrative/documentary or reporting findings with no physical observation (5 records) — e.g. GEREP volume mismatches, phase-vs-garanties-financières comparisons, unreported modification of exploitation conditions — where hunter's own G2 (non-physical) gravity already contradicted the M14 tag. 6) Domain over-labeling from adjacent-fiche bleed (D01_INCENDIE, D11_SECURITE, D10_ELECTRIQUE_ESP, D07_RETENTION — 5 records) where the constats_body has zero content matching the domain's taxonomy signals. 7) S01_CONSTAT/S02_INJONCTION stage-modifier inconsistency (6 records): several fiches had a formal demand with a numeric délai in constats_body but were staged S01_CONSTAT instead of S02_INJONCTION (or the reverse — S02_INJONCTION with m_DELAI already empty and no demand text at all, should be S01_CONSTAT). 8) R02_PROMESSE vs R14_NEUTRE_NC confusion (1 record): a claim of a completed-but-unproven PAST action was tagged as a FUTURE promise. No instances of the R10_TENSION/unfulfilled-promise pattern were found — R10_TENSION is not used anywhere in this batch (0 occurrences).
verdict: Hunter output is solid overall (0% M15, well-calibrated confidence flags — nearly all confirmed errors were self-flagged as medium confidence). The dominant error mode is systematic over-application of m_DELAI without checking for an actual numeric délai in the text, plus smaller multi-label bleed (domains/mechanisms) likely from adjacent fiches in the same multi-point inspection report. No code-validity violations (invalid ids, modifiers-in-mechanisms, multi-valued single fields) were found. Corrected 40 of 130 records (30.8%), all field-level (no wholesale re-classifications).
